sections.paragraph: [Asenath], Egyptian pr.n. of the daughter of Potipherah, priest of Heliopolis, whom Joseph married, Gen 41:45 46:20 . The LXX., whose authority has some weight in Egyptian names, write it Ἀσενέθ, MS. Alex. Ἀσεννέθ, which in Egyptian I would write ⲁⲥ-ⲛⲉⲓⲧ she who is of Neith (i.e. Minerva of the Egyptians), from ⲁⲥ (she is), like Asisi ⲁⲥ-ⲏⲥⲉ she who is (devoted) to Isis. Differently explained by Jablonsky in Opusc.ii.209; Panth. Ægypt.i.56. As to Neith, see also Champollion, Panthéon Egyptien, No. 6.
